---
title: Coffee grounds in the compost
date: 2024-06-04
tags: [gardening, cooking, coffee, compost]
---

Used coffee grounds count as a green in the compost pile despite their brown color, because they are high in nitrogen relative to their carbon content. I keep a lidded tub under the sink and empty it into the heap every few days along with filter papers and tea bags.

Straight coffee grounds on a garden bed can compact into a water-shedding crust, so I always mix them with dry leaves or shredded cardboard first. In a balanced pile they break down quickly and leave the finished compost a shade darker and noticeably richer.

My local roaster gives away sacks of spent grounds on Saturday mornings, far more than my household produces. A few extra sacks turn an underperforming pile into a hot one within a day, which is a free and cheerful way to speed up the heap.
